content.method: Multi-level statistical decomposition of segregation indices (e.g., Duncan dissimilarity, entropy measures) at primary versus secondary school transitions using national student registry data, tracking cohorts across both selection points.
content.question: What proportion of Hungary's segregation originates in primary-school choice and residential sorting occurring before age 10-11, versus at the gimnázium selection point itself, and would reforms targeting only the secondary-level selection point meaningfully reduce equity gaps?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether any of the proposals focused solely on the gimnázium entry gate (T1, T2, T3 without catchment reform) can achieve their stated equity goals, or whether primary-level mechanisms are the dominant driver requiring separate policy attention.
